security_gap.gap_description: Claim authorization depends on backend proof which might be forgeable or compromised.

actor_risk.description: Attacker gains unauthorized ability to claim incentives belonging to other users.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting the claim mechanism allows direct theft of user funds (incentive tokens).

assumptions: The backend system generates claim proofs used by the contract for authorization.
The proof generation or verification mechanism is flawed (e.g., forgeable, guessable, replayable).

mitigation.strategy: Strengthen claim proof generation and verification, bind proof to transaction details.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the incentive claim proof mechanism to claim incentive tokens belonging to another user from the BGT Incentive Distributor Contract.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator list data displayed on frontend

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend displays data received from RPC without integrity verification.

actor_risk.description: User trusts the frontend to display accurate information for decision-making.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Misleading validator or portfolio data can cause users to make financially detrimental decisions (e.g., boosting wrong validator).

assumptions: Attacker can compromise the Berachain Hub frontend or the data flow (Hub -> RPC) it relies on.
The frontend does not cryptographically verify the integrity of data received before display.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure secure frontend hosting, validate data integrity before display, use HTTPS.

description: An attacker compromises the Berachain Hub frontend to alter the data displayed to users (e.g., validator stats, portfolio), influencing their actions.

security_gap.gap_description: RPC node resources are limited and can be overwhelmed by high request volume.

actor_risk.description: Any external user can send requests to the public RPC endpoint.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disrupting the RPC node prevents users from interacting with the chain, including submitting transactions or querying data.

assumptions: The RPC endpoint lacks sufficient rate limiting or distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) protection.
An attacker can generate a high volume of requests targeting the RPC endpoint.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust rate limiting, deploy DDoS protection services, distribute RPC access points.

description: An attacker floods the public Berachain RPC endpoint with excessive requests, overwhelming its capacity and preventing legitimate users from accessing the blockchain.
